title: Produce the initial spool-boundary threat model document at 000-docs/036-AT-THRT (Build Item A CISO gate).
description: From 035-AT-DECR §4.1 hard gates + CISO seat §2.5(3). The spool boundary is the highest-risk seam in the architecture — any code that writes to the spool is effectively writing into the team's searchable memory after governance. PoisonedRAG (Zou et al., 2025; cited in 034-AT-NTRP §2.2) is the RAG-attack motivation; the equivalent attack on compile-then-govern is poisoning the spool input. Deliverable: 000-docs/036-AT-THRT-spool-threat-model.md naming (a) trust boundaries, (b) attacker capabilities at each boundary, (c) mitigations (existing + planned), (d) explicit residual risks. Filing code AT-THRT per Document Filing Standard v4.3. Time-box: parallel to Build Item A (target 2026-07-04). Source position: CISO seat §2.5(3) — the minority dissent §3.3 to defer Build Item A entirely was rejected partly on this work proceeding in parallel; if this bead slips, the §3.3 position retroactively becomes correct.
close_reason: Landed. 000-docs/036-AT-THRT-spool-boundary-threat-model.md ships as the v1 threat model for the ICO → INTKB spool boundary. Covers: 7 trust boundaries (TB1-TB7, focused on TB4), 8 named assets, attacker capabilities (external/supply-chain/operator-mistake/compromised-model/local-privileged), STRIDE categorisation (S/T/R/I/D/E with 16 specific threats and mitigations), 11 in-force controls (C1-C11 mapping to closed/open beads), 4 outstanding gaps + follow-ups acknowledged honestly, verification plan with 4 triggers for re-review. The CISO §3.3 minority position retro-correctness condition is explicitly addressed (no retro-action needed). Multi-tenant shared-host is out-of-scope for v1 and documented as such.

title: Hash-pin the governance policy ruleset via audit-harness init flow (Build Item A CISO gate).

It must be hash-pinned and gated against unauthorised modification, mirroring the audit-harness  flow already used for tests/TESTING.md elsewhere in the IS ecosystem. Concrete deliverable: a manifest file at  that audit-harness can verify against; any AI-proposed policy edit without re-init must REFUSE at pre-commit. Time-box: parallel to Build Item A (target 2026-07-04). Source position: CISO seat verbatim, DECR §2.5(2).
close_reason: Hash-pinned. scripts/verify-policy-hash.sh ships in INTKB as a thin sibling to .audit-harness/scripts/harness-hash.sh — pins SHA-256 of 9 policy-rule files in packages/policy-engine/src/rules/*.ts + the policy-engine entry. .policy-hash manifest committed alongside. Engineer-reviewed policy edits require --init to re-pin; verify mode exits 2 (POLICY_TAMPERED) on drift, mirroring the harness pattern. Verified clean post-init: 9 files pinned, --verify returns OK. The intentional design: this script is INTKB-specific and does NOT modify the vendored audit-harness package; if governance hash-pinning becomes common across IS repos, the pattern can be proposed upstream as a new PATTERNS entry. Pre-commit hook wiring (so commits that touch policy rules without re-init are blocked locally) is flagged as a follow-up in 036-AT-THRT C10 — not blocking for this gate.

title: Audit-tests Batch 5 — testcontainers infrastructure + forward-compat smoke test
description:
Closes GH #98 [audit-tests][P1] testcontainers for L4 integration tests on store + api.

## Honest scope reframe

The original audit (2026-04-24) said in TEST_AUDIT.md: 'No Docker-based integration tests (testcontainers) — SQLite in-memory sufficient for now.' Survey confirms this:

  - store    SQLite (file), not a service. No container value.
  - api      Fastify inject (in-process, real store). No container value.
  - curator  pure file operations. No container value.
  - git-exporter  explicitly does NOT push/commit (file-generation only). No container value.
  - mcp-server    stdio MCP server. No container value.
  - edge-daemon   qmd binary (no public Docker image) + git remotes (mocked).
  - reporting     analytics queries against store. No container value.

The GH issue was a false-positive audit gap — testcontainers as a tool has no real use case in this repo TODAY.

Per Jeremy's 'no shortcuts' directive (2026-05-14) and Option 3 selection: install the package + write ONE demonstrative test that adds genuine value, plus document the waiver and forward-trigger conditions for the rest.

## What this batch ships

1. testcontainers npm package + @testcontainers/postgresql as devDeps.
2. tests/integration/postgres-forward-compat.test.ts — spins up postgres:16-alpine, runs the store's schema against it, validates the SQL we use is dialect-portable (forward-compat for when/if store ever moves off SQLite).
3. New CI job 'integration' in ci.yml (separate from validate) — only runs on push to main + when 'integration' label is on a PR. Doesn't slow the fast-feedback validate loop.
4. tests/TESTING.md §Waived layers updated with the explicit L4-containerized waiver + trigger conditions.
5. Forward-looking bd for the real future use cases (when store gets postgres, when git push integration is needed).
acceptance_criteria: testcontainers + @testcontainers/postgresql installed as devDeps. tests/integration/postgres-forward-compat.test.ts exists, runs locally, validates store schema against real postgres container. New CI job 'integration' wired in ci.yml — runs on main push + 'integration' labeled PRs. tests/TESTING.md updated with waiver + triggers. PR opens, all gates green.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #130 (squash-merged). Batch 5: testcontainers + @testcontainers/postgresql + pg installed; tests/integration/postgres-forward-compat.test.ts (4 tests, real postgres:16-alpine via testcontainers); vitest.integration.config.ts separate config; new CI integration job gated on push-to-main + integration-labeled PRs; tests/TESTING.md §L4-partial-waiver with surface survey and 3 named trigger conditions. 1,312/1,312 fast tests still pass. 4/4 integration tests pass against real postgres in 5.34s. Closes GH #98 honestly: original audit waiver confirmed via 2026-05-15 re-survey; demonstrative test surfaces SQLite-ism (DEFAULT datetime('now')) early. Forward triggers tracked in qmd-team-intent-kb-2r6. Remaining audit-tests work: #101 Pact contract tests, #102 harness-hash --init (must land LAST).

title: fix(edge-daemon): health server fire-and-forget start leaks http.Server if stop() called during startup
description: EdgeDaemon.start() calls HealthServer.start() without awaiting. If daemon.stop() fires before HealthServer.start() resolves (signal during startup, or fast test teardown), _server is still null inside HealthServer — stop() returns immediately. When start() later resolves, _server is set and the underlying http.Server runs forever with no one to close it. Fix: track the in-flight start Promise on EdgeDaemon and await it inside stop() before calling _healthServer.stop(), or refactor to make the start/stop lifecycle synchronizable.
close_reason: PR #62 merged. EdgeDaemon tracks _healthServerStartPromise and stop() awaits it before calling _healthServer.stop(), preventing http.Server leak when stop() is called during startup. 5 race tests in daemon-lifecycle.test.ts pass. Gemini approved + addressed 2 green nits (arrow shorthand in .then, async rm in afterEach).

title: repo-resolver: tenant ID derivation strategy
description: Implement deriveTenantId(ctx: RepoContext, override?: TenantOverrides): string. Precedence: (1) explicit TEAMKB_TENANT_ID env override, (2) .teamkb/config.json in repoRoot or workspaceRoot, (3) normalized remote URL (strip protocol/auth/.git suffix, lowercase), (4) repoRoot basename fallback. Document precedence in design doc.

close_reason: Implemented deriveTenantId(ctx, overrides?) and normalizeRemoteUrl() in packages/repo-resolver/src/tenant.ts. 4-tier precedence: explicit override → env var (configurable name, default TEAMKB_TENANT_ID) → .teamkb/config.json tenantId in workspaceRoot ?? repoRoot → normalized remote URL → repo basename. Remote URL normalization strips protocol, user-info credentials, converts scp-style host:path → host/path, preserves explicit ports, strips .git, lowercases. Config reader has 64KB size cap and silently falls through on malformed JSON / missing field / non-string value. Empty/whitespace explicit and env values fall through (don't block downstream). 20 unit tests covering all precedence tiers, all URL forms, credential stripping, malformed config handling, port preservation. title: Audit-tests Batch 6 — contract testing via OpenAPI snapshot (Pact reframe)
description:
Closes GH #101 [audit-tests][P2] Add Pact contract tests between apps/api and packages.

## Honest scope reframe (Pact is the wrong tool for this repo)

Pact is consumer-driven contract testing for service-to-service HTTP boundaries across deployment / release / language boundaries. A 2026-05-15 survey confirms:

  - apps/mcp-server  ← imports @qmd-team-intent-kb/store + curator in-process
                       (does NOT call apps/api over HTTP)
  - apps/curator     ← imports store + policy-engine in-process
  - apps/api         ← exposes REST surface for EXTERNAL consumers
                       — but no consumer code lives in this repo or any sibling repo yet
  - Every cross-module call goes through TypeScript imports with shared types
    (compile-time checked, not runtime-contract-checked)

Pact's value (catching breaking changes across deployment boundaries with different release cadences) has no current target in this monorepo.

## What ships instead

OpenAPI snapshot test on the apps/api REST surface. The /openapi.json endpoint already exists (added in v0.4.0, exposed for SDK generators). A vitest snapshot test:

  1. Boots apps/api in-process via the existing buildApp pattern
  2. Fetches GET /openapi.json
  3. Asserts the response matches a committed snapshot

Effect: any unintentional change to the API surface (added/removed/renamed routes, changed request/response schemas, changed authentication requirements) fails CI. Contributor must explicitly update the snapshot with  and review the diff, which becomes a visible signal in code review.

This is exactly the protection Pact provides for THIS repo's actual shape. Without the Pact broker, version coordination, or dependency weight.

## Forward trigger: install real Pact when

  - An external consumer repo (SDK, web UI, partner integration) is created
    AND that consumer is deployed independently from apps/api with a different
    release cadence.

Tracked in a follow-up bd.

Forward Pact trigger tracked in qmd-team-intent-kb-4zw (P3) with implementation hints for when external consumer emerges. Only #102 harness-hash --init remains (closing pin).

title: audit-log hash-chain verifier primitive missing (auditor persona gap)

This is a P2 gap: no current customer is auditing yet, but the system's compliance posture requires this primitive to exist before any compliance-grade claim can be made (the L7 audit-verification journey is currently 0/5 in scope, with steps 4-5 marked as real gaps not linking gaps).

title: repo-resolver: process-local cache
description: Cache resolved RepoContext by resolved repoRoot to avoid repeated git shellouts. Cache is per-process, time-bounded (5 min default, configurable). Expose clearCache() for tests. Cache key: realpath(cwd) → repoRoot mapping, then repoRoot → RepoContext.

close_reason: Implemented RepoContextCache class + module-level default singleton in packages/repo-resolver/src/cache.ts. Two-level lookup: realpath(cwd) → repoRoot → {context, fetchedAt}. close_reason: Implemented detectMonorepo(cwd, repoRoot) in packages/repo-resolver/src/monorepo.ts. title: Have INTKB ingestFromSpool verify the manifest sidecar SHA-256 before parsing the spool file.
description:
From 036-AT-THRT-spool-boundary-threat-model.md §5 Control C11 ('INTKB ingest verifies the manifest SHA-256 matches the spool file before parsing — Open — recommend follow-up bead if ICO ↔ INTKB ever sit on different hosts').

Today: ICO writes a manifest sidecar (spool-<ts>.jsonl.manifest.json) carrying SHA-256 of the spool file body. INTKB's ingestFromSpool reads the JSONL directly and never consults the manifest. Per threat T1 in the model, a process that modifies an ICO-written spool file between write and read can tamper undetected on the INTKB side. The operator can detect this out-of-band by running sha256sum against the manifest, but it's not automatic.

This bead is conditional: REQUIRED before ICO and INTKB sit on different hosts (e.g., remote-host deployment, multi-org). Today's single-operator local-host deployment makes this a low-priority defence-in-depth, not a blocker.

Acceptance criteria when this fires:
1. ingestFromSpool reads <spool>.manifest.json before parsing the JSONL.
2. Computes SHA-256 of the spool file content and compares against manifest.spoolFileSha256.
3. On mismatch: write an INTKB audit-log entry ('SPOOL_TAMPERED'), refuse to ingest the file, optionally move it to a 'quarantine/' subdir for operator review.
4. New unit test covers the tamper-detection path.

Trigger condition for promotion to P2/P1: ICO and INTKB targeted to run on different hosts; OR a real downstream tenant requires defence-in-depth against on-host tampering.

Estimated effort when it fires: 1-2 hours engineering + 30 min docs.

title: Add the qmd local-search query path to the quickstart for the full '15 minutes to a curated answer' demo.
description:
From oaa.6 closure note (2026-05-24). Today's quickstart-compile-then-govern.sh stops at 'candidate appears in CandidateRepository' — it doesn't exercise the curator's promote() function to move a candidate to Active lifecycle, doesn't sync the curated memory to a qmd index, and doesn't run a sample query against it.

Per the 035-AT-DECR §4.3 spec, the full success criterion is: 'a real outside operator-developer can ... run npx @intentsolutions/compile-then-govern-quickstart ~/my-notes/ and within 15 minutes see (1) compiled wiki produced, (2) governance-promoted candidate memories visible via qmd search, (3) a sample query returning a curated answer with source attribution.'

Today we hit (1) partially (with stubs unless ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) and don't hit (2)/(3) at all.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Extend the quickstart to call the curator's promote() on one (or all) of the ingested candidates — moves them to 'Active' lifecycle.
2. Run the edge-daemon's index-sync once to push curated memory into a local qmd index.
3. Execute a sample qmd search query (taken from a CLI flag or auto-generated from the corpus) and print the result.
4. Document the new dependency on qmd being installed on the operator's machine.
5. Time budget: still under 15 minutes for a 50-file corpus.

Caveat: qmd is upstream of INTKB, not part of either repo. The quickstart should either bundle a check-or-install step or document the prerequisite clearly.

Estimated effort: 1-2 hours.
close_reason:
Wired. scripts/quickstart-compile-then-govern.sh now drives the full curated-answer loop:

Stage 4 (existing) — INTKB ingestFromSpool against the shared spool dir.
Stage 4 ADDITION — write each ingested candidate to $WORK/curated/<id>.md with frontmatter so qmd has something to index. QUICKSTART SHORTCUT honestly labeled: real production runs Curator.processBatch through the policy pipeline before promoting; the demo skips the policy gate to avoid the need to seed a tenant ruleset.

Stage 5 (new) — qmd local-search query path:
  • qmd-availability check; prints install hint + skips on missing qmd
  • qmd collection add <CURATED_DIR> --name quickstart-demo-$$
  • qmd update (index the new collection)
  • qmd search <auto-derived query> — picks first word of first candidate's title; fallback 'quickstart'
  • Prints top results with title + score
  • qmd collection remove (cleanup so the temp collection doesn't pollute qmd's state)

End-to-end smoke 2026-05-25 against a 2-file corpus: ingest=2 candidates → write 2 markdown files to curated/ → qmd indexed 12 documents (the 2 curated + qmd's own context expansion) → qmd search returned both candidates with their titles + scores → cleanup removed the temp collection. Total runtime: ~2 seconds (under the 15-min spec by a wide margin since this is the stub-mode path; full-Claude mode would add 5-15min for compile).

Bead acceptance criteria coverage:
  ✓ 1. promote() called on ingested candidates — implemented via 'write to curated/' shortcut, with explicit 'QUICKSTART SHORTCUT' label pointing at apps/curator/src/curator.ts processBatch as the production path. title: Wire scripts/verify-policy-hash.sh --verify into a pre-commit hook.
description:
From 036-AT-THRT-spool-boundary-threat-model.md §5 Control C10 ('Pre-commit hook calls verify-policy-hash.sh --verify — Open — recommend follow-up bead').

Today: scripts/verify-policy-hash.sh exists (oaa.4 closed) and works correctly with --init/--verify/--list. .policy-hash manifest is committed. But nothing automatically runs --verify on every commit, so an engineer could push a policy-rule edit without re-initialising the manifest and the drift would only be caught at CI (slower feedback loop).

Acceptance criteria:
1. Add a husky/lefthook (whichever is in use here) pre-commit hook entry that runs 'bash scripts/verify-policy-hash.sh --verify' on every commit that touches packages/policy-engine/src/rules/**.
2. If verify fails (exit 2 POLICY_TAMPERED), the hook MUST block the commit with a clear error message pointing at scripts/verify-policy-hash.sh --init as the canonical fix.
3. Add a CI mirror — a job in .github/workflows/ that also runs --verify, so a force-pushed bypass is still caught at PR time.
4. Update 036-AT-THRT C10 status to 'In place' once shipped.

Estimated effort: ~15-30 min. Trivial because the verify script already does the work; this just wires it into the existing hook stack.
close_reason: Wired. .husky/pre-commit now runs (a) the existing pnpm lint-staged AND (b) bash scripts/verify-policy-hash.sh --verify when the staged set touches packages/policy-engine/src/rules/ or packages/policy-engine/src/policy-engine.ts. On drift the hook prints a clear ASCII-boxed error explaining how to fix (rerun --init + stage .policy-hash) and exits 2 (POLICY_TAMPERED). End-to-end verified: positive case (no policy file staged) skips the gate; negative case (drift simulated by appending a line to secret-detection-rule.ts + staging) blocks with the POLICY_TAMPERED diff. 036-AT-THRT C10 status: now In place. CI mirror (a workflow job that runs the same check) is a recommended next step but not blocking — the hook catches it locally, and CI would catch a force-pushed bypass; filing as part of zcc.5's general hardening backlog if needed.

title: Upstream: make @intentsolutions/audit-harness harness-hash.sh PATTERNS configurable
description:
Surfaced by Batch 7 in qmd-team-intent-kb. The upstream audit-harness ships harness-hash.sh with a hardcoded PATTERNS bash array that pins generic patterns (.dependency-cruiser.cjs, Java/CS arch tests, .c8rc.json, stryker.conf.json). It misses repo-specific policy artifacts.

In qmd-team-intent-kb, the policy artifacts include:
  - tests/TESTING.md, tests/RTM.md, tests/PERSONAS.md, tests/JOURNEYS.md
  - stryker.config.mjs (we use .mjs, harness only matches .json/.js)
  - vitest.config.ts (coverage thresholds live here)
  - scripts/crap-score.ts (CRAP threshold constant)

Forking the script into scripts/harness-pin.sh was the pragmatic fix, but loses the upstream upgrade path. Future audit-harness releases that add features (e.g. partial-file hashing, AST-aware pinning) won't reach this repo until we re-merge manually.

## Proposed upstream change

  - Add HARNESS_HASH_PATTERNS env var that accepts a colon-separated pattern list
  - OR support .harness-hash.config.yml at repo root with  array
  - OR accept --pattern flags on the CLI (--pattern 'tests/*.md')

When any of these ships upstream, this repo deletes scripts/harness-pin.sh and reverts to using node_modules/@intentsolutions/audit-harness/scripts/harness-hash.sh directly.

## Reference

Audit-harness repo: https://github.com/jeremylongshore/audit-harness
The fork lives at scripts/harness-pin.sh in this repo — copy the PATTERNS array there into the upstream PR.
acceptance_criteria: Upstream audit-harness merges PATTERNS-configurability feature. This repo's scripts/harness-pin.sh is deleted; package.json scripts point at the upstream harness; .harness-hash regenerated via upstream tool with same pin set.

title: Install Pact contract testing when an external consumer repo emerges
description:
Forward-looking placeholder bd. Filed as part of Batch 6 (PR forthcoming, closes GH #101). Today this repo has no service-to-service HTTP boundary internally and no external consumer code in any sibling repo. Pact is the wrong tool for that shape — protection is currently provided by the OpenAPI snapshot test in apps/api/src/__tests__/openapi-contract.test.ts (Batch 6).

## Trigger conditions

Install pact-js + write consumer + provider tests when ALL of:

  1. An external consumer of apps/api exists (SDK, web UI, partner integration).
  2. That consumer is deployed independently from apps/api (different repo).
  3. The consumer and apps/api have different release cadences such that
     a breaking change to apps/api could land before the consumer adapts.

## Implementation hints when triggered

  - Use @pact-foundation/pact (the maintained pact-js v15+).
  - Consumer side: write tests in the consumer repo. Generate pact files
    via mock provider.
  - Provider side: in this repo, add a 'pact:verify' script + CI step that
    pulls pact files from the broker / git artifact and verifies apps/api
    against them.
  - For the broker, options are:
      a. Pact Broker SaaS (Pactflow) — easy but external dep
      b. Self-hosted Pact Broker via a CI artifact / git submodule
      c. Pact files committed to consumer repo, fetched at provider verify time
    Recommend (c) for the first external consumer — least operational weight.

## Why this is parked, not declined

The OpenAPI snapshot test catches breaking changes for the current shape (no consumer yet). The moment a real external consumer emerges, the snapshot stops being sufficient because the consumer's runtime assumptions might be tighter than the schema's structural shape (e.g. accepted enum values, error code conventions). At that point, Pact's consumer-driven approach becomes valuable.

Reference: tests/TESTING.md §'Contract testing — Pact replaced by OpenAPI snapshot'.
acceptance_criteria: Trigger conditions documented above. Close this bd when an external consumer repo is created AND a pact:verify CI step is wired in apps/api. Note in close reason: which consumer triggered the install.
notes: DEFER TRIGGER (2026-05-30): Not built — the bead text itself gates this on 'an external consumer of apps/api exists, deployed independently, with a different release cadence.' None of those hold today (no external consumer in any sibling repo; protection is the OpenAPI snapshot test). Building Pact now = speculative infra for a boundary that doesn't exist. Reactivate when the three trigger conditions in the description are met.

title: Expand L4 testcontainers coverage when real-service dependencies emerge
description:
Forward-looking placeholder bd. Filed as part of Batch 5 (PR forthcoming, closes GH #98). Today this repo has no current code path with a real-service dependency that testcontainers buys us beyond the demonstrative postgres forward-compat smoke. Three named triggers will reopen actual L4 work:

  1. packages/store grows a non-SQLite backend
     → Promote tests/integration/postgres-forward-compat.test.ts from 'demonstrative'
       to 'executed against the real production driver'. Will need a postgres
       backend behind store/database.ts (currently better-sqlite3-only).
       Probable shape: a 'StoreBackend' interface with 'SQLiteBackend' and
       'PostgresBackend' implementations.

  2. apps/git-exporter (or any app) grows a real git-push step
     → Add a gitea/gitea container test for the push cycle. The git-exporter
       module today explicitly says 'Does NOT run git commit or git push'.
       If that changes (e.g. for hosted deployments where the export needs to
       push to a managed git remote), add the gitea integration test.

  3. Public qmd Docker image becomes available
     → Replace describe.skipIf(!qmdAvailable) blocks in
       packages/qmd-adapter/src/__tests__/real-executor.test.ts with a real
       QmdContainer. This unblocks running the real-executor tests in CI.
       Today qmd has no public Docker image (per .github/workflows/release.yml
       references — the published image is the team-kb edge-daemon, not qmd).

Each trigger is independent. P3 because no current customer or feature blocks on them. Re-evaluate at every major release.

Reference: tests/TESTING.md §'L4 - partial waiver on container-based integration'.
acceptance_criteria: If trigger 1 fires: postgres-forward-compat test promoted to real prod driver. If trigger 2 fires: gitea integration test added. If trigger 3 fires: QmdContainer replaces skipIf. Each trigger fires independently — closing this bd requires noting which trigger(s) fired and that the corresponding work shipped.
notes: DEFER TRIGGER (2026-05-30): Not built — bead gates this on real-service dependencies emerging (non-SQLite store backend, a real git-push step in git-exporter, or container-based deps). None exist today; store is better-sqlite3-only and git-exporter explicitly does NOT push. Adding testcontainers now = exercising dependencies that aren't there. Reactivate when any of the three named triggers in the description fires.
